title: 第 50 節
en: ' Yes, there is something better than will.'
1 Sir, tell it me.'
Fifth Khanda.

1. ' Consideration (/£itta)2 is better than will. For when a man considers, then he wills, then he thinks in his mind, then he sends forth speech, and he 1 This paragraph is obscure. The text seems doubtful, for instance, in samak/Zpatam, samakalpetam, and samakalpatam. Then the question is the exact meaning of sa»*k//ptyai, which must be taken as an instrumental case. What is intended is that, without rain, food is impossible &c. or inconceivable; but the text says, ' By the will of rain food wills/ &c. Will seems almost to be taken here in the sense in which modern philosophers use it, as a kind of creative will. By the will of rain food wills, would mean, that first rain wills and exists, and afterwards the vital airs will and exist, &c.
2 -fttfta, thought, implies here consideration and reflection.
[3l I 114 ATFANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.
sends it forth in a name. In a name the sacred hymns are contained, in the sacred hymns all sacrifices.

2. ' All these (beginning with mind and ending in sacrifice) centre in consideration, consist of consideration, abide in consideration. Therefore if a man is inconsiderate, even if he possesses much learning, people say of him, he is nothing, whatever he may know; for, if he were learned, he would not be so inconsiderate. But if a man is considerate, even though he knows but little, to him indeed do people listen gladly. Consideration is the centre, consideration is the self, consideration is the support of all these. Meditate on consideration.

3. ' He who meditates on consideration as Brahman, he, being himself safe, firm, and undistressed, obtains the safe, firm, and undistressed worlds which he has considered ; he is, as it were, lord and master as far as consideration reaches — he who meditates on consideration as Brahman.'
' Sir, is there something better than consideration ?' tion.'
1 Sir, tell it me.'
Sixth Khanda.

1. ' Reflection (dhyana)1 is better than consideration. The earth reflects, as it were, and thus does the sky, the heaven, the water, the mountains, gods and men. Therefore those who among: men obtain 1 Reflection is concentration of all our thoughts on one object, ekagrata. And as a man who reflects and meditates on the highest objects acquires thereby repose, becomes firm and immovable, so the earth is supposed to be in repose and immovable, as it were, by reflection and meditation.

VII PRAPArffAKA, 7 KHAA'DA, 2. I 1 5 greatness here on earth, seem to have obtained a part of the object of reflection (because they show a certain repose of manner). Thus while small and vulgar people are always quarrelling, abusive, and slandering, great men seem to have obtained a part of the reward of reflection. Meditate on reflection.
2. ' He who meditates on reflection as Brahman, is lord and master, as it were, as far as reflection reaches — he who meditates on reflection as Brahman.'
' Sir, is there something better than reflection ? '
'Yes, there is something better than reflection.'
' Sir, tell it me.'
Seventh Khaada.

1. 'Understanding (vi^fiana) is better than reflection. Through understanding we understand the ivYg-veda, the Ya^ur-veda, the Sama-veda, and as the fourth the Atharvawa, as the fifth the Itihasapurawa l, the Veda of the Vedas, the Pitrya, the Ran, the Daiva, the Nidhi, the Vakovakya, the Ekayana, the Deva-vidya, the Brahma-vidya, the Bhuta-vidya.the Kshatra-vidya, the Nakshatra-vidya, the Sarpa and Deva^ana-vidya, heaven, earth, air, ether, water, fire, gods, men, cattle, birds, herbs, trees, all beasts down to worms, midges, and ants ; what is right and what is wrong ; what is true and what is false ; what is good and what is bad ; what is pleasing and what is not pleasing ; food and savour, this world and that, all this we understand through understanding. Meditate on understanding.
2. 'He who meditates on understanding as Brahman, reaches the worlds where there is understanding 1 See before, p. 109. I 2 Il6 tftfANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.
and knowledge 1 ; he is, as it were, lord and master as far as understanding reaches — he who meditates on understanding as Brahman.'
' Sir, is there something better than understanding?' 'Yes, there is something better than understanding.' ' Sir, tell it me/ Eighth Kha2vda.

i. ' Power (bala) is better than understanding. One powerful man shakes a hundred men of understanding. If a man is powerful, he becomes a rising man. If he rises, he becomes a man who visits wise people. If he visits, he becomes a follower of wise people. If he follows them, he becomes a seeing, a hearing, a perceiving, a knowing, a doing, an understanding man. By power the earth stands firm, and the sky, and the heaven, and the mountains, gods and men, cattle, birds, herbs, trees, all beasts down to worms, midges, and ants ; by power the world stands firm. Meditate on power.
2. ' He who meditates on power as Brahman, is, as it were, lord and master as far as power reaches — he who meditates on power as Brahman.'
1 Sir, is there something better than power ? '
' Yes, there is something better than power.'
' Sir, tell it me.'
Ninth Khajvda.

i. ' Food (anna) is better than power. Therefore if a man abstain from food for ten days, though he live, he would be unable to see, hear, perceive, think, act, and understand. But when he obtains The commentator takes vi^fiana here as understanding of sacred books, ^-fiana as cleverness with regard to other subjects.
zh: 「是的，有些東西比意志更好。」
先生，請告訴我。」
第五篇章

「考量（*citt*a）比意志更好。因為當一個人考量時，他才意欲，他在心中思考，他才發出言語，而他」¹ ¹ 本段落晦澀難解。文本似有問題，例如在 samak/Zpatam、samakalpetam 和 samakalpatam 等處。其次，問題在於 sa»*k//ptyai 的確切含義，必須理解為工具格。本意似乎是：沒有雨，食物就不可能等等，或無法想像；但文本說的是「由雨之意志食物意欲」等等。意志在此幾乎是以現代哲學家所使用的意義來理解，即某種創造性的意志。「由雨之意志食物意欲」將意味著，首先雨意欲而存在，其後生命之氣意欲而存在，等等。
2 -fttfta（思想）在此意為思量與反省。
[3l I 114 愛坦多奧義書（Aitareya-Upanishad）。
將其以一個名稱發送出去。在一個名稱中包含著神聖的讚歌，在神聖的讚歌中包含著所有的祭祀。
2. 「所有這些（始於心智，終於祭祀）皆以思慮為中心，皆由思慮構成，皆安住於思慮。因此，如果一個人缺乏思慮，即使他擁有豐富的學問，人們也會說他一無是處，不論他知道什麼；因為若他確有學問，就不會如此缺乏思慮。但若一個人具有思慮，即使他知識甚少，人們也會樂於聆聽他。思慮是中心，思慮是自我，思慮是所有這些的支柱。思考思慮吧。」
3. 「凡以思慮為梵天而冥想者，彼既自身安全、堅固、無患，則獲得安全、堅固、無患之諸世界，乃其所思慮者；彼如同為主宰與統治者，其範圍至於思慮所及——凡以思慮為梵天而冥想者是也。」
「先生，有什麼事情比得上深思熟慮嗎？」
「先生，請告訴我。」
第六章
「冥想（dhyana）勝於思考。大地如同冥想一般，天空、天界、水、山嶺、諸神與人類亦復如是。因此，凡人中得以獲得」 1 冥想乃將我們一切思想集中於一個對象，謂之一境（ekagrata）。正如一個人若冥想沉思於最高的對象，由此獲得安寧，變得堅定而不可動搖；同樣地，大地也被認為通過冥想與沉思，如同處於安寧與不可動搖的狀態。
七 普拉帕答迦（PRAPAÑAKA）、七 卡雅達（KHAA'DA）、二‧一一五 在此塵世獲得偉大者，似乎已得到了思慮對象的一部分（因為他們展現出某種沉著的態度）。因此，當小人物與庸俗之輩總是互相爭執、辱罵與詆毀時，偉大的人物似乎已獲得了思慮之報酬的一部分。應當冥想思慮。
2. 「凡冥想將反思視為梵天者，則為主人與統治者，儘管其統治範圍僅達反思所及之處——凡冥想將反思視為梵天者。」
'先生，有什麼東西比思辨更好嗎？'
「是的，有比反思更好的東西。」
'先生，請告訴我。'
第七卡塔（Seventh Khaada）。

「理解（vi^fiana）勝於思辨。透過理解，我們理解梨俱吠陀、夜柔吠陀、娑摩吠陀，以及作為第四部的阿闥婆吠陀，作為第五部的《往世書》（Itihasapurawa），即吠陀之吠陀、祖靈之學、王權之學、神之學、寶藏之學、言語之學、一一瑜伽之學、提婆之學、梵學、物質之學、剎帝利之學、星宿之學、蛇與神祇之學；天、地、空、以太、水、火、諸神、人類、牲畜、禽鳥、草本、樹木、一切獸類，直至蟲蠅與螞蟻；何為正義，何為不義；何為真實，何為虛妄；何為善，何為惡；何為悅意，何為不悅意；食物與滋味，此世與彼世——一切，我們都透過理解而理解。當思惟理解。」
2. 「誰若靜慮於以梵為智慧者，即到達有智慧之世界」¹ ¹ 參見前文，第109頁。 ² 《歌者奧義書》（Chandogya-Upanishad）。
及知識¹；他在某種意義上是主宰與統領，凡理解所及之處——那沉思於以梵天（Brahman）為理解本質之人。
「先生，有比理解更好的東西嗎？」「有的，有比理解更好的東西。」「先生，請告訴我吧。」第八卡班陀（Eighth Kha2vda）。
i. 「力量（bala）優於理解。一個有力量的人撼動百個有理解的人。若人有力量，他便成為上升之人。若他上升，他便成為親訪智者之人。若他親訪，他便成為追隨智者之人。若他追隨他們，他便成為觀察、聆聽、感知、認識、行動、理解之人。由於力量，大地堅定不移，天空、天界、群山、諸神與人類、牲畜、鳥類、草本、樹木、所有獸類直至蟲、蚊蠓與螞蟻；由於力量，世界堅定不移。當冥想力量。」
2. 「誰若冥想力量乃梵（Brahman），則如同君主與主人，其範圍至於力量所及之處——誰若冥想力量乃梵。」
先生，難道有什麼東西比權力更美好嗎？
『是的，有某些東西比權力更美好。』
「先生，請告訴我。」
第九梨俱吠陀讚歌
i. 「食物（anna）勝於力量。因此，若一人十日不進食，雖然活著，卻無法看見、聽聞、感知、思考、行動與理解。但當他獲得...」註釋者在此處將 vi^fiana 理解為對聖典之領悟，^-fiana 為對其他學科之聰慧。
